videos.hook_text: Which animal could crush a bowling ball in its jaws?
videos.notes: Kept the ground-row opener (only rows layout across this 3-video batch) but replaced the buggy narrow bowling-ball 'stack' kit element with a new crush-pile mechanic (croc jaw + 4x2 bowling-ball grid) after finding its title text overlapped the '3,700 psi' number.

timeline.detail: Which animal could crush a bowling ball in its jaws?
top-biters row build starts, crocodile photo rises first
3,700 PSI lands on the crocodile row
cut to pressure-gauges: human vs crocodile dials
Our bite is just 160 PSI — a crocodile bites 23 times harder!
23x STRONGER label lands between the two gauges
cut to crush-pile: croc jaw photo + bowling-ball grid
3,700 PSI is enough force to crush seven bowling balls in a single bite!
7th bowling ball lands, = 7 bowling balls caption completes
No machine humans have ever built can bite this hard — evolution did it for free. / Which of these bites surprised you the most?

videos.hook_text: The hottest planet isn't the one closest to the Sun!
videos.franchise: The Planet Myth That Is Wrong
videos.notes: Replaced the ground-row opener (which would have been the 3rd identical row layout in this batch) with a new heat-tubes mechanic: 5 planet thermometers filling cold-blue to scorching-red. Fixed a bug where the 900F/800F labels floated up into the planet photos when a tube was nearly full.

timeline.detail: The hottest planet isn't the one closest to the Sun!
heat-tubes build starts, Venus tube fills red first
900 F lands inside the Venus tube
cut to Venus Furnace: globe fills and glows
Venus is so hot its surface glows — 900 degrees, day and night!
cut to orbit_group: Order From the Sun
Mercury sits closest, but Venus's thick clouds trap the most heat!
all 4 planets settle into their orbital rings
Your oven maxes out at 500F. Venus is hotter than that everywhere, all the time, forever. / Did Venus beating Mercury surprise you?

videos.hook_text: This animal is louder than a jet engine taking off!
videos.notes: Replaced the ground-row opener with a new decibel-bars equalizer mechanic (photo riding each volume bar, pulsing sound-wave ticks on the loudest) so this video reads as 3 distinct sound-themed depictions instead of repeating the animal-row pattern already used in bite-force-champions this batch.

timeline.detail: This animal is louder than a jet engine taking off!
decibel-bars build starts, sperm whale bar rises with pulse ticks
230 dB lands on the sperm whale bar
cut to shockwave-duel: sperm whale vs jet engine
A jet engine roars at 150 dB — a sperm whale is far louder!
1.5x LOUDER label lands between the rings
cut to epicenter: sperm whale centered, rings reach the frame edges
At 230 decibels, the sperm whale is the loudest animal that has ever lived!
the loudest animal EVER caption lands as rings hit the frame edges
That click is so loud, scientists think it could stun or deafen anything nearby. / Which animal's volume shocked you?
